## validateFormSpecElements() function

Validates FormSpec elements against constraints.

This is the main entry point for validating a form specification against a constraint configuration. It walks through all elements and checks each one against the configured constraints.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export declare function validateFormSpecElements(
  elements: readonly FormElement[],
  options?: FormSpecValidationOptions
): ValidationResult;
```

**Returns:**

[ValidationResult](./constraints.validationresult.md)

Validation result with all issues found

## Example

```ts
import { formspec, field, group } from "@formspec/dsl";
import { validateFormSpecElements, defineConstraints } from "@formspec/constraints";

const form = formspec(
  group("Contact", field.text("name"), field.dynamicEnum("country", "countries"))
);

const result = validateFormSpecElements(form.elements, {
  constraints: {
    fieldTypes: { dynamicEnum: "error" },
    layout: { group: "error" },
  },
});

if (!result.valid) {
  console.error("Validation failed:", result.issues);
}
```
